title: What's new this year? 2024
description: New initiatives or legislation that may affect the individual tax return or supplementary tax return 2024.
keywords: what is new, new in Individual tax return instructions, paper tax return, paper form, paper return, paper lodgement

content_blocks.text: From 1 July 2023, the cents per kilometre rate is 85c for work-related car expenses . If you're using the cents per kilometre method use this rate in income year 2024.

From the 2024 income year, if you received one or more distributions from trusts, you must complete Trust income schedule 2024 and attach it to your tax return. The trust income schedule details each distribution that you receive from trusts.
Certain amount you report in your trust income schedule are amounts you report in your supplementary tax return at questions:

items: 20 Foreign source income and foreign assets or property.

content_blocks.text: For information to help you complete the trust income schedule and who must complete the schedule, see Trust income schedule and instructions 2024 .
For more information, see Modernising trust administration systems .
Effective life determination for depreciating assets
We're updating how we publish effective life determinations.

Use the effective life of a depreciating asset to work out its decline in value. You can either make your own estimate of its effective life or use the Commissioner's effective life determinations. For assistance with both, see Effective life of an asset .
From the 2024 income year, you claim all eligible deductions for work-related self-education expenses at question D4 Work-related self-education expenses .
In prior income years you would claim formal education courses provided by professional associations, seminars, education workshops or conferences at question D5 Other work-related expenses .
This doesn’t change the types of expenses or deduction you can claim, only the question where you claim them.
This change is limited to expenses you claim at question D4 and D5 .
You continue to claim motor vehicle and travel expenses at questions D1 and D2 .

items: For more information on changes that may impact individuals this tax time, see What's new for individuals .

Return to main menu Individual tax return instructions 2024 .

content_text:
## Cents per kilometre rate
From 1 July 2023, the cents per kilometre rate is 85c for work-related car expenses . If you're using the cents per kilometre method use this rate in income year 2024.
## Trust income schedule
From the 2024 income year, if you received one or more distributions from trusts, you must complete Trust income schedule 2024 and attach it to your tax return. The trust income schedule details each distribution that you receive from trusts.
Certain amount you report in your trust income schedule are amounts you report in your supplementary tax return at questions:
- 13 Income
- 18 Capital gains
- 19 Foreign entities
- 20 Foreign source income and foreign assets or property.
For information to help you complete the trust income schedule and who must complete the schedule, see Trust income schedule and instructions 2024 .
For more information, see Modernising trust administration systems .
## Effective life determination for depreciating assets
We're updating how we publish effective life determinations.
Use the effective life of a depreciating asset to work out its decline in value. You can either make your own estimate of its effective life or use the Commissioner's effective life determinations. For assistance with both, see Effective life of an asset .
## Self-education deductions
From the 2024 income year, you claim all eligible deductions for work-related self-education expenses at question D4 Work-related self-education expenses .
In prior income years you would claim formal education courses provided by professional associations, seminars, education workshops or conferences at question D5 Other work-related expenses .
This doesn’t change the types of expenses or deduction you can claim, only the question where you claim them.
This change is limited to expenses you claim at question D4 and D5 .
You continue to claim motor vehicle and travel expenses at questions D1 and D2 .
## Where to go next
- For more information on changes that may impact individuals this tax time, see What's new for individuals .
- Go to Do you need the supplementary tax return instructions 2024? .
- Return to main menu Individual tax return instructions 2024 .
